skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Guides agents through strict test-driven development for features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.
skill.summary: Apply red-green-refactor discipline before writing implementation code.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and common words as external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Review found these pattern hits are false positives because the skill contains documentation and test examples, not executable scripts or network logic. A medium data-integrity concern remains because the skill repeatedly instructs agents to delete code when TDD was not followed.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Workflow Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to delete implementation code and start over when tests were not written first. This supports TDD discipline, but it can cause data loss if followed without user confirmation or version-control checks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive instruction is explicit and repeated in the main skill file. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the surrounding context is TDD process guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside Markdown fences, diagrams, TypeScript examples, and illustrative test commands. No evidence found of executable scripts, command construction, or user-controlled shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed locations are static text or examples in Markdown. There is no runnable Ruby, shell wrapper, or instruction to execute arbitrary commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to prose or ordinary test example text, not cryptographic functions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, custom encryption, or password hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions, table text, or sample cleanup method names. The file has no cryptographic implementation or dependency use.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are references to testing concepts such as mocks and setup complexity. No evidence found of host discovery, port scanning, network probing, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are part of a TDD checklist or anti-pattern documentation. They do not request network access, environment enumeration, or system commands.

content.value_statement: Teams often ship code with tests that only confirm an existing implementation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through red-green-refactor work before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to write a failing test before implementation.
Provides red, green, and refactor checkpoints for feature work.
Defines rules for verifying test failures and clean test output.
Explains when mocks are useful and when they hide behavior.
Supplies checklists for bug fixes, refactoring, and test quality.
limitations: It does not include executable automation or test runner integration.
It cannot choose the correct framework without project context.
It may be too strict for prototypes or generated code.
Deletion guidance should be applied only with user approval.

use_cases.title: Feature Development
use_cases.description: Write the expected behavior as a failing test before adding production code.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Verification
use_cases.description: Reproduce a bug with a failing test, then implement the smallest passing fix.

use_cases.title: Test Quality Review
use_cases.description: Review mock-heavy tests and replace brittle assertions with behavior-focused checks.

prompt_templates.title: Start a TDD Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill for this feature. First identify one behavior, write the failing test, and wait for confirmation before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug with TDD
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill to fix this bug. Create a failing regression test that reproduces the issue before changing production code.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill for this refactor. Confirm the relevant tests pass first, keep behavior unchanged, and run tests after each step.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Mock Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill and testing anti-pattern guidance to review these tests. Identify mocks that hide real behavior and propose safer alternatives.

output_examples.input: Add retry logic for a failing operation.
output_examples.output: The assistant starts by naming one retry behavior, writes a failing test for three attempts, verifies the failure, then implements minimal passing logic.
The assistant creates a regression test for an empty email, confirms it fails for the expected reason, and then adds the smallest validation change.

output_examples.input: Review a test that asserts a sidebar mock exists.
output_examples.output: The assistant flags the assertion as testing the mock, then recommends checking visible navigation behavior or using the real component.

best_practices: Confirm each new test fails for the expected reason before implementation.
Keep each test focused on one observable behavior.
Use real dependencies unless a mock is necessary and well understood.
anti_patterns: Writing production code before a failing test exists.
Asserting that a mock was rendered instead of checking user-visible behavior.
Adding test-only methods to production classes.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and example commands, but the agent must use the project test runner.

faq.question: Can it work with any test framework?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is framework-neutral, though examples use TypeScript-style tests.

faq.question: Is this only for new features?
faq.answer: No. It also applies to bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.

faq.question: When should the skill not be used?
faq.answer: It may not fit throwaway prototypes, generated code, or configuration-only changes.

faq.question: How does it handle mocks?
faq.answer: It encourages real behavior tests first and treats mocks as isolation tools, not test targets.

faq.question: Is the deletion guidance safe?
faq.answer: It should be used carefully. Agents should confirm with the user before deleting uncommitted or valuable work.
